objective.primary: Convert the existing AI agent course outline into a deterministic instructional sequence where Mental Models for Agent Architecture is Module 1 and the education analogy is extended as the throughline across all modules.
objective.instructional_goal: Teach AI agent architecture, prompt engineering, task analysis, machine-legible contracts, human-agent relationship norms, contracting versus prompting, verification, evaluation, and domain-specific agent design through the teacher-student-classroom-homework-report-card analogy.

course_sequence.title: Mental Models for Agent Architecture
course_sequence.function: Establish the teacher, student, classroom, textbook, homework, and report card analogy as the course grammar.

course_sequence.title: Task Analysis: The Lesson Plan Beneath Every Prompt
course_sequence.function: Teach task analysis as the foundation and substrate of all prompt engineering.

course_sequence.title: Bilingual Systems: Human-Legible and Machine-Legible Instruction
course_sequence.function: Teach when to use human-readable prose, machine-legible schema, and paired bilingual documents.

course_sequence.title: Human-Agent Relationships: Trust, Priority, and Communication
course_sequence.function: Teach that human-agent interaction creates relationship dynamics requiring trust, commitment, clear priorities, mutual respect, and clear communication.

course_sequence.title: Contracting vs. Prompting: The Minimal Contract Window
course_sequence.function: Teach the difference between prompting and contracting, introducing the Verification and Truth statement as a minimal Contract Window.

course_sequence.title: The Sequester Contract
course_sequence.function: Introduce constrained execution and contamination control after learners understand contracts.

course_sequence.title: Building Pro-Grade Evaluation Rubrics
course_sequence.function: Extend V&T from minimal truth reporting into evaluation rubrics.

course_sequence.title: Deterministic Evals
course_sequence.function: Teach repeatable evaluation as stable grading rather than subjective assessment.

course_sequence.title: Design, Build, Run
course_sequence.function: Teach the agent lifecycle as curriculum design, classroom execution, grading, and revision.

course_sequence.title: Culminating Project: Build a Domain-Specific Agent with V&T
course_sequence.function: Require learners to build a domain-specific agent with task analysis, bilingual instruction, contract boundaries, knowledge base, tool plan, evaluation rubric, and V&T statement.

repo_system.name: v0-individualized-instruction-model
repo_system.implementation_stack: Next.js 15.5.15 + React 19 + TypeScript 5 + Tailwind 4 + ShadCN + Vercel

included: Reorder the instructional sequence so Mental Models for Agent Architecture is Module 1.
Create five new module specifications using the existing app voice, tone, instructional style, and educational analogy.
Preserve existing module concepts for The Sequester Contract, Building Pro-Grade Evaluation Rubrics, Deterministic Evals, and Design, Build, Run.
Map each module explicitly to the education analogy.
Define learning objectives, key takeaways, practice prompts, and capstone deliverables.
Produce paired human-readable and machine-legible course artifacts.
Produce verification evidence showing course-order integrity, analogy-throughline consistency, required-module coverage, and V&T inclusion.
excluded: Changing authentication, payments, deployment configuration, analytics, or unrelated app infrastructure.
Replacing the product brand, voice, or visual system.
Removing existing accessibility controls visible in the current learning page.
Claiming learner efficacy without evaluation data.
Claiming production deployment without build, test, and deployment evidence.

baseline_state.known_existing_voice: clear, analogy-driven, direct, practitioner-oriented instructional prose
baseline_state.known_existing_analogy: teacher, student, textbook, classroom, homework, report card

content_dependencies: Existing Mental Models for Agent Architecture module content
Existing Pro-Grade Evaluation Rubrics module content
Existing Design, Build, Run module content

operations.name: resequencing_existing_advanced_modules
operations.note: Resequencing encoded in sequence manifest; lib/course-data.ts not modified (integration pass required)

invariants.name: mental_models_first
invariants.rule: Mental Models for Agent Architecture must be Module 1.

invariants.name: ten_module_sequence
invariants.rule: The final course sequence must contain exactly ten modules.

invariants.name: five_new_modules_present
invariants.rule: The five requested new modules must exist.

invariants.name: education_analogy_throughline
invariants.rule: Every module must explicitly map its core concept to the education analogy.

invariants.name: existing_voice_preserved
invariants.rule: New module prose must preserve the existing app voice.

invariants.name: accessibility_controls_not_removed
invariants.rule: The build must not remove existing learner-facing accessibility controls.

invariants.name: human_machine_legibility_pair
invariants.rule: The course sequence must include both a human-readable Markdown guide and a machine-legible JSON sequence manifest.

invariants.name: vt_statement_required
invariants.rule: The contracting module and capstone module must teach and require a Verification and Truth statement.

invariants.name: no_false_deployment_claim
invariants.rule: The build must not claim deployment, learner testing, or production validation without evidence.

invariants.name: no_markdown_only_enforcement
invariants.rule: No enforceable obligation may exist only in Markdown without corresponding machine-legible representation.

halt_matrix.condition: Mental Models for Agent Architecture is not Module 1
final course sequence does not contain exactly ten modules
any requested new module is missing
any module lacks explicit education-analogy mapping
contracting module or capstone module omits V&T statement requirements
build claims production deployment, learner validation, or efficacy without evidence

acceptance_criteria: The contract preserves the C-RSP v4.0 core structure.
The final sequence places Mental Models for Agent Architecture as Module 1.
The final sequence contains exactly ten modules.
The five requested new modules are present and named clearly.
Existing modules are resequenced after the new contracting foundation without being erased from the course arc.
Every module includes an explicit education-analogy mapping.
The course sequence includes paired human-readable and machine-legible artifacts.
The contracting module introduces Verification and Truth statement as a minimal Contract Window.
The capstone requires a domain-specific agent with V&T statement.
The build does not remove existing learner-facing accessibility affordances.
The build produces evidence files for sequence integrity, analogy-throughline coverage, V&T presence, and content changes.
The build does not claim production deployment or learner efficacy without evidence.

rollback.safe_state: baseline commit 3fca0cae59d4a7769ca442008b5125986464bbd5 with no generated course-sequence artifacts applied
procedure: Stop execution immediately when any halt condition is active.
Do not mutate constitutional authority files.
Do not mutate files outside allowed_roots.
Restore modified course files to baseline commit.
Remove generated files listed under artifacts.generated_paths only if rollback is invoked before acceptance.

not_claimed_boundary: This contract does not claim the course has been implemented in the app.
This contract does not claim the app has been built or deployed.
This contract does not claim learner outcomes have been tested.
This contract does not claim accessibility has been audited beyond preservation of existing visible controls.
This contract does not claim verifier module hashes are frozen (hashes are computed at runtime).
This contract does not claim a remote repository exists.
